open_points.question: May a report be published after quorum wake when not all expected child identities are completely and cleanly reconciled?
Do failed or timed-out expected child identities block reporting, or may reporting proceed with those identities marked unresolved?
Does a reported unknown identity force rejection, or may it be ignored or separately surfaced while reporting proceeds?
If duplicate child representations appear, must reporting reject or may one representation be accepted?

scenarios.expected: After 6 expected children succeed, quorum k=6 is satisfied and the control plane wakes; this is join readiness only, not a complete report.
Two expected child identities reach terminal status failed and are counted in accounting; they remain unresolved for any complete label until business failure policy is decided.
One expected child identity reaches terminal status timed_out and is counted in accounting; it remains unresolved for any complete label until business failure policy is decided.

One returned identity is not present in the launch snapshot; final reconciliation detects an unknown identity and the batch cannot satisfy complete-label checks while unknown handling remains unresolved.
If partial reporting is refused, no final report is published because reconciliation has unresolved expected identities and an unknown identity despite quorum wake.
If partial reporting is allowed, a non-complete partial report may be published after quorum wake with 6 succeeded, 2 failed, 1 timed_out, and 1 unknown identity explicitly surfaced.

open_points.question: What control-plane wake condition should trigger final synthesis for the best-report-possible outcome: explicit deadline, no-further-progress signal, manual trigger, or another rule?
If the same work-item identity is represented more than once, should the workflow reject the batch, pick one result, or surface duplicates in a partial report?
If a worker returns an identity not present in the 50-item launch manifest, should that result be rejected, ignored, or included with warning?
Which failed, cancelled, or timed-out work items may be ignored when producing the final report, if any?
May an incomplete final report be published, and if so what omissions or unresolved identities are acceptable and how should the report be labelled?

scenarios.expected: all 50 expected identities are represented exactly once in terminal statuses; reconciliation passes all checks and final synthesis can be labelled complete
if one or more expected identities are not represented when the control plane wakes, reconciliation marks unresolved identities and any publish decision remains governed by the open partial_policy
failed identities are counted in terminal status accounting and included in unresolved identities for final reconciliation
timed_out identities are counted in terminal status accounting and included in unresolved identities for final reconciliation
duplicate represented identities are detected by reconciliation and prevent a complete label unless the duplicate_policy is later defined otherwise
results for identities outside the 50-item launch manifest are detected by reconciliation and flagged for unknown_policy handling
if a future business decision allows partial publication, the control plane can wake under the chosen wake_when rule, reconcile unresolved identities, and pass a partial summary to final synthesis
